---
title: "README_Project3"
output: html_document
---

---
title: "README_Project3"
output: html_document
---

Project 3

Repository structure

Background/ – project description / references  
Code/ – analysis scripts  
DataRaw/ – raw data folder  
DataProcessed/ – cleaned/derived datasets used in the analysis  
Reports/ – report source files and knitted outputs  

Code files created:

Code/Primary_Analysis_Project3.Rmd – primary analysis script for Project 3, including cohort construction, exploratory summaries, Kaplan-Meier analyses, Cox proportional hazards modeling with LASSO-based variable selection, proportional hazards diagnostics, and profile-specific 10-year stroke probability prediction  
Reports/Project3_Report.Rmd – Project 3 report source file  
README_Project3.Rmd – project README file  

Data files used

DataRaw/frmgham2.csv – raw Framingham teaching dataset used for Project 3 analyses  

Processed data files created

DataProcessed/analytic_period1_10yr.rds – baseline Period 1 analytic cohort after exclusion of prevalent stroke, with 10-year stroke outcome and follow-up variables  
DataProcessed/analytic_period1_10yr.csv – csv version of the baseline analytic cohort for inspection  
DataProcessed/longitudinal_baseline_cohort_periods123.rds – longitudinal dataset containing Periods 1, 2, and 3 records for the same baseline-eligible cohort  
DataProcessed/cohort_counts.rds – saved cohort counts used for reporting and cohort summaries  
DataProcessed/model_completeness_summary.rds – summary of completeness for core and candidate model variables  

Completed the following components of the Project 3 analysis:

Loaded the raw Framingham dataset:  
DataRaw/frmgham2.csv  

Constructed the baseline analytic cohort:
restricted the dataset to Period 1 baseline observations  
excluded participants with prevalent stroke at baseline  
defined 10-year stroke event status  
defined 10-year follow-up time with administrative censoring at 10 years  

Created processed datasets for downstream analysis:
saved the baseline analytic cohort to DataProcessed/analytic_period1_10yr.rds  
saved the baseline analytic cohort as a csv for inspection  
saved the longitudinal Period 1–3 dataset for the same baseline-eligible cohort  
saved cohort count summaries and model-completeness summaries  

Performed exploratory data analysis:
summarized missingness in baseline variables  
reviewed continuous-variable ranges and flagged extreme values for inspection  
checked binary variable coding  
examined whether missingness in BPMEDS, TOTCHOL, and BMI was associated with 10-year stroke outcome  
summarized the baseline cohort overall and by sex  
created Table 1 with baseline characteristics, missingness, follow-up summaries, and 10-year Kaplan-Meier stroke-free survival  
created Table 2 showing change in selected risk factors across examination periods in the same baseline-eligible cohort  
examined distributions of continuous variables by sex  
evaluated associations between systolic blood pressure and blood pressure medication use  
examined correlations among continuous candidate predictors  
performed bivariate exploratory comparisons of candidate predictors with 10-year stroke outcome  

Performed unadjusted survival analyses:
created Kaplan-Meier cumulative incidence curves over the first 10 years of follow-up  
compared survival patterns overall by sex  
compared survival patterns within sex by:
diabetes  
current smoking  
blood pressure medication use  
prevalent coronary heart disease  
categorized systolic blood pressure  
computed log-rank test results for the prespecified Kaplan-Meier comparisons  

Performed the primary Cox proportional hazards analysis:
fit sex-specific Cox proportional hazards models for 10-year incident stroke  
forced age, systolic blood pressure, and diabetes into the models a priori  
considered the following additional candidate predictors:
current smoking  
blood pressure medication use  
prevalent coronary heart disease  
total cholesterol  
body mass index  
used LASSO with 5-fold cross-validation and lambda.min for variable selection  
used the union of selected predictors across the male and female models for the final refit models  
refit standard Cox proportional hazards models separately in men and women  
created Table 3 with adjusted hazard ratios, 95% confidence intervals, and p-values by sex  

Assessed proportional hazards assumptions:
computed Schoenfeld residual tests for each final Cox model  
reviewed global and term-specific proportional hazards test results  
generated Schoenfeld residual plots  
generated log-minus-log survival plots for binary predictors in the final models  

Computed profile-specific 10-year stroke probability predictions:
defined five prespecified baseline risk profiles  
evaluated predictions at ages 40, 50, and 60  
used sex-specific Cox models to estimate 10-year stroke probability and 95% confidence intervals  
created Table 4 with predicted 10-year stroke probability by sex, age, and risk profile  
created a figure showing predicted 10-year stroke probability across ages and profiles  

Report files:
Reports/Project3_Report.Rmd – report source  
Reports/[knitted report output to be added] – final knitted report output once rendered
